At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Malformed Tree Fixtures model · Theory Malformed Tree Fi…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 8919 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove tokenizer versus tree choice, goquery documents, malformed repair, browser differentials, and budgets; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test implied elements, misnesting, tables, templates, foreign content, comments, duplicate attributes, and deep input.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Malformed Tree Fixtures model · Theory Malformed Tree Fi…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Malformed HTML returns a parser error instead of a repaired tree.

checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
